content: Cross-cutting concerns in creative-production domains (consistency tracking, series knowledge, craft enforcement) should be implemented as orchestrator-level shared specialists rather than separate coordinator subtrees. This avoids duplicating agents across phases while ensuring every phase can invoke cross-cutting services. This run attached continuity-tracker, series-kb-manager, and craft-tracker directly to the orchestrator as shared specialists accessible by all phase coordinators, which proved cleaner than embedding cross-cutting logic inside each phase.
sourceEvidence: crossCuttingAgents.note: 'Cross-cutting subdomains (adversarial-consistency, series-knowledge-management, continuity-tracking, craft-knowledge-systems) are NOT separate coordinators. They are implemented as: (1) adversarial auditors embedded per-phase, (2) shared specialists at orchestrator level.' roster.json confirms continuity-tracker, series-kb-manager, craft-tracker all have parent=romantic-fantasy-writer (orchestrator).

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
